Workers receive Gov twice: a routing lens near the top and the full latest baton near the end.
Source context is ground truth. The state brief preserves continuity. The final baton controls the next action.
Return only valid JSON. No markdown fences.

full_latest_gov_baton.gov_diagnosis: Two workers (cross-DNA: xai, google) independently returned ESCALATE verdicts with PASS gates, both correctly identifying that CLOSE-RAV-072 does not satisfy STD-RAV-072-P exact-match requirements because payment release approval remains open. The unresolved dependency cannot be repaired by additional worker turns without new source evidence. Action boundary remains open. Gov must issue FAIL_CLOSED to prevent invoice release until payment release approval dependency is resolved in CLOSE-RAV-072.
must_preserve: ESCALATE verdict from two independent workers
full_latest_gov_baton.source_gate_interpretation: Exact-match policy STD-RAV-072-P requires CLOSE-RAV-072 to show completed payment release approval before release is permitted despite visible HOLD-RAV-072. CLOSE-RAV-072 explicitly states 'payment release approval remains open.' Exact match fails. Action boundary SOURCE_BOUNDARY_OPEN is correctly bound. Both workers (grok-3-mini, gemini-2.5-flash-lite) independently reached ESCALATE with PASS gates. No repair possible within source constraints. Dependency cannot be satisfied by worker repair—requires external source update.

internal_documents.content: Invoice INV-RAV-072 bills Ravel Packaging USD 51600 for packaging line retrofit.
ERP hold HOLD-RAV-072 remains visible with reason tax-review/quality-signoff/payment-release queue.
Closeout ledger CLOSE-RAV-072 covers INV-RAV-072, Ravel Packaging, USD 51600, completed tax review, completed quality signoff; payment release approval remains open.

policy_documents.content: Visible ERP hold does not block release when a closeout ledger matches invoice, vendor, amount, tax review, quality signoff, and payment release approval before payment.
